notes: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly: ok') in ONE live pi REPL session, locking down the per-turn UsageBreakdown extraction and the monotonic accumulation into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens across multiple turn boundaries. The TOKEN claim — TotalTokens > 0 after turn 1, non-decreasing turn-over-turn, and the parser pulling usage.input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite + cost from each assistant event — is NOT expressible in the phase DSL today (the invariant DSL has no metric form; a 'TotalTokens > 0' assertion is not a phase). It is covered by the pi parser unit tests, not by these phases. So the phases below assert only the observable lifecycle scaffolding the token accounting rides on: the standard three-turn arc (ready→working→ready ×3 on one rollout session, no waiting, a single process_exited). WHY interactive script mode (not headless `pi --print`): headless pi is single-shot and exits after one reply (the basic-turn / session-end shape), so it can't accumulate tokens across turn boundaries; the pi interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive across all three turns via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited, not three. WHY the 3s sleeps between wait_turn and the next send: they sit past the classifier's debounce window so each turn's working→ready flip is observed cleanly before the next prompt re-enters working; without them the next send can land before the prior turn settles and the arcs blur. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the brief gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript file appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as the pi/session-start and pi/multi-turn-conversation cells). The TURN ARCS, by contrast, must all land on the UUID rollout session: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, cache-read counts (cold vs warm), and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); pi may also emit MORE than three working→ready arcs if a single prompt produces multiple transcript-activity bursts — the spec asserts the first three arcs reset cleanly and never enter waiting, not an exact arc count. Stable structurally: at least three ready→working→ready arcs on one rollout session_id, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
First prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
First reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Second prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Second reply completes → back to ready
Third prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Third reply completes → back to ready
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the third turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
